title: piptocarpha verticillata
intro: Piptocarpha verticillata, also known as the white-top or cotton crown, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and soothing sore throats. The herbal preparations of Piptocarpha verticillata typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or taken as capsules.
